questions.question: What number should replace the question mark in this sequence: 1, 3, 4, 7, 11, 18, 29, ?
What letter comes next in the sequence: A D H K O ?
OBFUSCATE : CLARITY
questions.justification: Obfuscate removes clarity; confuse removes understanding

questions.question: What is shared_ptr in C++?

answers.A1: A pointer that is always shared between functions
answers.A2: A smart pointer that allows multiple pointers to share ownership of same object
answers.A3: A pointer that cannot be copied
answers.A4: A pointer used only in shared libraries

questions.key: A smart pointer that allows multiple pointers to share ownership of same object
questions.justification: shared_ptr uses reference counting to manage shared ownership, deleting the object when the last pointer is destroyed.

questions.question: What is the purpose of catch(...)?

answers.A1: To catch all exceptions as a last resort
answers.A2: To create a default catch block

questions.key: To catch all exceptions as a last resort
questions.justification: catch(...) is a catch-all handler that catches any exception type not caught by specific catch blocks.

questions.question: What is dynamic polymorphism?

answers.A2: Polymorphism resolved at runtime through virtual functions and inheritance

questions.key: Polymorphism resolved at runtime through virtual functions and inheritance
questions.justification: Dynamic polymorphism (runtime) uses virtual functions to determine which overridden method to call based on actual object type at execution time.

questions.question: Complexity analysis in algorithms refers to which of the following?

answers.A2: Study of algorithm performance and resource usage

questions.key: Study of algorithm performance and resource usage

questions.question: What is an adjacency list representation of a graph?

answers.A1: List of adjacent vertices in order
answers.A2: Collection of lists showing edges for each vertex
answers.A3: Ordered list of all graph elements

questions.key: Collection of lists showing edges for each vertex
questions.justification: Adjacency list efficiently represents sparse graphs

questions.question: What distinguishes a priority queue from a regular queue in terms of element extraction?

answers.A1: All elements have the same priority
answers.A2: Elements are extracted based on priority
answers.A3: Only the first element is priority

questions.key: Elements are extracted based on priority
